rulings.Case Title: NYLIM REAL ESTATE VS. FRANK LEMBI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Charge Member'S Interest In Limited Liability Companies; Memo Of P & A And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 19, 2012, Line 14, PLAINTIFF NYLIM REAL ESTATE MEZZANINE FUND II, LP'S Motion For Order To Charge Member's Interest In Limited Liability Companies. Granted as to 1090 Eddy Street, LLC, 501 Lincoln Way, LLC, FEL, LLC, Lem/Ray Properties, LLC, Skyline Enterprises, LLC, and Skyline Investments, LLC; and denied without prejudice to being re-filed as to Personality Hotels, LLC, 500 Larkin DE, LLC, Bay Citi Properties DE, LLC, Citi Properties DE, LLC, Citi Properties I DE, LLC, Franklin/Sutter DE, LLC, Ritz Apartments DE, LLC, Skyline Enterprises DE, LLC, Trophy Properties DE, LLC, Trophy Properties VIII, LLC, Trophy Properties VX, LLC. Defendant did not oppose the motion as to 1090 Eddy Street, LLC, 501 Lincoln Way, LLC, FEL, LLC, Lem/Ray Properties, LLC, Skyline Enterprises, LLC, and Skyline Investments, LLC. Plaintiff served Personality Hotels, LLC at an incorrect address and may re-file the motion as to that entity if service is properly made. As to the remaining entities, plaintiff does not now have sufficient evidence that defendant has an ownership interest in those entities, but may re-file the motion if it obtains such evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOHN ARCHIE MERRILL, AS TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN VS. COURNALE & CO. (CAL DRE LICENSE NO. 00322534) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cournale & Co. (Cal Dre License No. 00322534), Clyde Cournale, Curtis Cournale, William Schmetterer'S Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Supplemental Designation Of Expert Witnesses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 19, 2012, Line 7, Defendants Cournale & Co. (Cal Dre License No. 00322534), Clyde Cournale, Curtis Cournale, William Schmetterer's Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Supplemental Designation Of Expert Witnesses. The court adopts the recommendation and rationale of Judge Pro Tem Halperin as follows: Denied. Although Plaintiff's narrative concerning Mr. Blanchard's testimony is not a model of specificity, it gives sufficient notice of what Mr. Blanchard will say at trial to allow Defendants to assess whether to take Mr. Blanchard's deposition, to fully explore the relevant subject area at any such deposition, and "gear up to cross-examine" Mr. Blanchard effectively. Nor is Mr. Blanchard's designation impermissibly duplicative of the anticipated subjects of Plaintiff's previously-designated expert Mr. Littlefield. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN ARCHIE MERRILL, AS TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN VS. COURNALE & CO. (CAL DRE LICENSE NO. 00322534) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 19, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANTS COURNALE & CO. (CAL DRE LICENSE NO. 00322534), CLYDE COURNALE, CURTIS COURNALE, WILLIAM SCHMETTERER DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the third and fourth causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth and sixth causes of action. The third and fourth causes of action are adequately pled. As to the fifth cause of action, the provision in the Lender/ Purchaser Disclosure Statement is not an agreement to be bound by Civil Code 2941.9 but is simply a disclosure that section 2941.9 may apply. Furthermore, the signature on the Lender/ Purchaser Disclosure Statement only demonstrates an acknowledgement of receipt of the disclosure and not an agreement of any kind. Given that Plaintiff has not alleged any other written agreement, it is unlikely that further leave to amend can cure these deficiencies. The request for judicial notice is denied. As to the sixth casue of action, despite being given leave to do so, Plaintiff has not alleged any cognizable basis for the claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN ARCHIE MERRILL, AS TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN VS. COURNALE & CO. (CAL DRE LICENSE NO. 00322534) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 19, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANTS COURNALE & CO. rulings.Case Title: TRANSITION, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. WB COYLE A/K/A W.B. COYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Resposnes, To Enfroce The Court'S Discovery Order; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 19, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFF TRANSITION, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY, JAMES BAILEY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, To Enforce The Court's Discovery Order; And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear and advise Court re Defendants' statement in moving papers that further discovery responses will have been served by the time of the hearing. Plaintiffs to prepare proposed order enumerating discovery responses that remain at issue. Due to delayed production of further discovery responses mandated by the Court's order dated July 18, 2012, Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $2,475. The Court did not receive a courtesy copy of the Reply brief. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: RASHID ALATRAQCHI VS. METRO CAB LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Suash Subpoenas; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 19, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 3, DEFENDANT METRO CAB LLC, RICHARD HYBELS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas; Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion to Quash Plaintiff's Subpoenas issued August 21, 2012 is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to comply with the requirements of Cal. Code Civ. Proc. section 2020.410 - the subpoenas at issue do not command compliance ¿ on a date that is no earlier than 20 days after the issuance, or 15 days after the service, of the deposition subpoena, whichever date is later. Defendants' request for sanctions in DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT
